title: Mars University

jokes.text: - What field? - I don't care. The pay's the same.
jokes.setup: Professor mentions Nobel Prize experiment, Fry asks what field
jokes.punchline: Fry's indifference to academic achievement, only caring about pay
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Fry's blue-collar indifference to prestige is both funny and character-defining

jokes.text: Much like Utah. But unlike Utah, it was eventually made livable...
jokes.setup: Mars described as dreary, uninhabitable wasteland
jokes.punchline: Comparing it to Utah, then saying Mars improved but implying Utah didn't
jokes.explanation: Classic Futurama geographic burn with perfect structure - setup Utah comparison, then twist the knife

jokes.text: They planted traditional college foliage: ivy, trees, hemp.
jokes.setup: Traditional college foliage being planted
jokes.punchline: Hemp casually included as 'traditional' college plant
jokes.explanation: Good list joke with hemp as the unexpected third item, though fairly predictable

jokes.text: - What was your minor? - Robo-American Studies.
jokes.setup: Bender majored in Bending at college
jokes.punchline: His minor was 'Robo-American Studies' - a play on ethnic studies programs
jokes.explanation: Brilliant worldbuilding joke - takes real academic concept and makes it robot-specific with perfect economy

jokes.text: - Close enough. Come on in. - Thanks! Here's your finger back.
jokes.setup: Bender being mistaken for moose-head fumigator
jokes.punchline: Casually returning someone's finger like it's normal
jokes.explanation: Great throwaway gag - implies Bender did something terrible off-screen but treats it casually

jokes.text: Uh-oh! Nerds!
jokes.setup: Robot fraternity playing chess
jokes.punchline: Robots calling each other nerds despite being the nerdiest possible activity
jokes.explanation: Nice irony of nerdy robots being embarrassed by their nerdiness, though predictable

jokes.text: ...and crammed 58 humans into a phone booth.
jokes.setup: Recounting Bender's legendary college antics
jokes.punchline: Absurdly high number of humans crammed in phone booth
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of college pranks with robot-level absurdity

jokes.text: Yeah, well, a lot of them were children.
jokes.setup: 58 humans in phone booth sounds impossible
jokes.punchline: Bender's casual justification that children are smaller
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender moment - casually dark justification delivered with complete nonchalance. Peak character writing.

jokes.text: Even Hillel has better parties.
jokes.setup: Robot fraternity being the lamest on campus
jokes.punchline: Being outdone by Jewish student organization known for being quiet/studious
jokes.explanation: Good college reference joke playing on Hillel's reputation for being academic rather than party-focused

jokes.text: Good old Coney Island College. Go Whitefish!
jokes.setup: Fry reminiscing about college
jokes.punchline: Nonsensical college name and mascot
jokes.explanation: Random absurdity that fits Fry's character but lacks strong comedic structure

jokes.text: Everyone knows 20th-century colleges were really expensive daycare centers.
jokes.setup: Fry showing off his college dropout certificate
jokes.punchline: Dismissing 20th century higher education as just expensive daycare
jokes.explanation: Sharp satirical observation about higher education that feels contemporary and biting

jokes.text: Math of wonton burrito meals. I'll be there!
jokes.setup: Professor says 'quantum neutrino fields'
jokes.punchline: Fry hears it as 'wonton burrito meals'
jokes.explanation: Classic Fry mishearing that's both character-appropriate and actually sounds plausible

jokes.text: Please, I don't know how to teach. I'm a professor.
jokes.setup: Fry eager to take the professor's class
jokes.punchline: Professor admitting professors don't actually know how to teach
jokes.explanation: Brilliant academic satire with perfect economy - captures the reality of research vs teaching in higher ed

jokes.text: Bingo! - Oh, mama!
jokes.setup: Robots presumably looking at something exciting
jokes.punchline: Robots getting excited about something mundane (implied to be bingo)
jokes.explanation: Weak visual gag that's hard to parse from transcript alone

jokes.text: Oh, yeah. Someone's been a bad computer.
jokes.setup: Context unclear from transcript
jokes.punchline: Bender talking dirty to a computer
jokes.explanation: Character-appropriate but relies on sexual innuendo without clear setup

jokes.text: I say! You've damaged our servants' quarters. And our servants!
jokes.setup: Complaining about property damage
jokes.punchline: Treating servant injury as an afterthought to property damage
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing upper-class callousness through priority ordering

jokes.text: This time, Robot House has gone too far!
jokes.setup: Damage to servants' quarters
jokes.punchline: Dean's dramatic declaration about Robot House
jokes.explanation: Standard authority figure reaction, sets up Animal House parody

jokes.text: Cheese it!
jokes.setup: Getting caught by authority
jokes.punchline: Bender's signature 'run away' phrase
jokes.explanation: Character consistency callback that works in context

jokes.text: Brilliant deduction! You're a credit to your species.
jokes.setup: Fry stating the obvious that his roommate is a monkey
jokes.punchline: Monkey sarcastically praising Fry's intelligence
jokes.explanation: Good character introduction - establishes Guenter's intelligence and condescension

jokes.text: Oh, please, that's preposterous science-fiction mumbo-jumbo.
jokes.setup: Being asked if monkey is genetically engineered
jokes.punchline: Dismissing genetic engineering as sci-fi nonsense in a sci-fi show
jokes.explanation: Meta joke about the show's own genre while setting up even more ridiculous explanation

jokes.text: You're wasting your breath. He didn't understand a word.
jokes.setup: Professor explaining complex science
jokes.punchline: Monkey insulting Fry's intelligence
jokes.explanation: Continues establishing Guenter's superiority complex over Fry

jokes.text: I understood the word "hat."
jokes.setup: Guenter saying Fry understood nothing
jokes.punchline: Fry's pathetic defense of understanding one simple word
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry moment - simultaneously defending himself while confirming his stupidity

jokes.text: I prefer banana-flavored energy bars made from tofu.
jokes.setup: Fry offering banana to monkey
jokes.punchline: Pretentious alternative that defeats the purpose of a banana
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Guenter rejecting his nature for artificial sophistication

jokes.text: Look to your left, then to your right, then in nine other directions.
jokes.setup: Traditional 'look left and right' college warning
jokes.punchline: Adding impossible 'nine other directions'
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of familiar college trope with sci-fi twist

jokes.text: Boring. Let's hear about Walter Mondale already.
jokes.setup: 20th century history class introduction
jokes.punchline: Student eager to learn about famously boring politician
jokes.explanation: Good reference joke about Mondale's reputation for being dull

jokes.text: What do you call it? Lite Brite!
jokes.setup: Asked about 20th century broadcasting device
jokes.punchline: Fry confidently wrong with toy name instead of television
jokes.explanation: Classic Fry wrong answer that sounds plausible in his mind

jokes.text: What I love about being dean... is the peace and quiet and the respect I receive.
jokes.setup: Dean expressing satisfaction with his job
jokes.punchline: Immediately interrupted by chaos, proving him wrong
jokes.explanation: Standard dramatic irony setup for immediate contradiction

jokes.text: - When a human corpse is desecrated- - I can explain that.
jokes.setup: List of Robot House infractions escalating
jokes.punchline: Bender casually claiming he can explain corpse desecration
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender moment - matter-of-fact about horrific acts, very character-specific

jokes.text: Robot House is on dodecatupple-secret probation!
jokes.setup: Animal House reference to double-secret probation
jokes.punchline: Escalating to mathematically absurd 'dodecatupple'
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of famous Animal House reference with sci-fi math terminology

jokes.text: My mom is gonna kill me!
jokes.setup: Robots in trouble with authority
jokes.punchline: Robot worried about disappointing his mother
jokes.explanation: Humanizing robots with parental anxiety is mildly amusing

jokes.text: Fat-Bot! No!
jokes.setup: Dean treasuring his model ship
jokes.punchline: Fat-Bot presumably eating/destroying it
jokes.explanation: Visual gag that's hard to evaluate from transcript, relies on character's eating habit

jokes.text: Not even if you were the last man on Mars.
jokes.setup: Guenter asking female student to kegger
jokes.punchline: Classic rejection line modified for Mars setting
jokes.explanation: Standard phrase with location substitution, predictable but functional

jokes.text: I got her number! How do you like them bananas?
jokes.setup: Guenter celebrating getting a girl's number
jokes.punchline: Using banana idiom while being an actual monkey
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific pun - monkey using banana idiom with double meaning

jokes.text: I got her number! How do you like them bananas?
jokes.setup: Guenter being rejected by girl
jokes.punchline: Misinterpreting rejection as success, with banana pun
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Guenter's social cluelessness despite intelligence

jokes.text: Therefore, by process of elimination... the electron must taste like grapeade.
jokes.setup: Scientific reasoning process
jokes.punchline: Completely nonsensical conclusion about electron flavor
jokes.explanation: Good Professor Farnsworth moment - scientific method leading to absurd conclusion

jokes.text: That monkey kept me up all night with his constant thinking.
jokes.setup: Fry being late to class
jokes.punchline: Complaining about roommate thinking too loudly
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist complaint that perfectly captures Fry's anti-intellectual nature

jokes.text: If you don't stop fighting, I'll have you both neutered.
jokes.setup: Professor trying to stop Fry and Guenter's conflict
jokes.punchline: Threatening to neuter them like pets
jokes.explanation: Dark but character-appropriate threat from mad scientist treating humans like lab animals

jokes.text: That'll show him.
jokes.setup: Professor threatening to neuter both of them
jokes.punchline: Fry thinking this will somehow hurt Guenter more
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry logic - missing that he'd also be neutered

jokes.text: - How much more for Phi Beta Kappa? - How much you got?
jokes.setup: Parents asking about academic honors
jokes.punchline: Dean's mercenary response about buying honors
jokes.explanation: Good satire of academic corruption and wealthy parents buying achievement

jokes.text: This is so humiliating!
jokes.setup: Guenter's parents acting like wild monkeys
jokes.punchline: His embarrassment at his family's behavior
jokes.explanation: Relatable family embarrassment but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Now these monkeys I like!
jokes.setup: Professor dealing with intelligent vs wild monkeys
jokes.punchline: Preferring the wild ones to the sophisticated one
jokes.explanation: Professor's contrarian nature but not particularly strong

jokes.text: It may sound like an after-school special... but we learned who the real animal was today.
jokes.setup: Moral lesson about Robot House behavior
jokes.punchline: Self-aware reference to after-school special moralizing
jokes.explanation: Meta-commentary on the show's own moralizing, moderately clever

jokes.text: Look at me. My folks were honest, hardworking people.
jokes.setup: Fry trying to comfort Guenter about family
jokes.punchline: Fry implying he turned out badly despite good parents
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Fry's self-awareness about disappointing his parents

jokes.text: They can't. It's all the hat!
jokes.setup: Fry noting monkeys can't cry
jokes.punchline: Guenter explaining his tears are artificial intelligence
jokes.explanation: Clever explanation that adds depth to the character's existential crisis

jokes.text: He's not your father. That guy in the punch bowl was your father.
jokes.setup: Guenter calling Professor father-like
jokes.punchline: Brutally pointing out his real father acting wild at the party
jokes.explanation: Harsh but effective reality check that drives the character's decision

jokes.text: Why didn't I break his legs?
jokes.setup: Professor lamenting Guenter running away
jokes.punchline: Wishing he'd physically disabled him to prevent escape
jokes.explanation: Perfect Professor moment - treating intelligent being like lab equipment

jokes.text: All he handed in was a paper smeared with feces. He tied with Fry.
jokes.setup: Describing Guenter's degraded academic performance
jokes.punchline: Revealing this still matched Fry's performance
jokes.explanation: Brutal but perfect punchline that perfectly captures Fry's academic abilities

jokes.text: He belongs in the wild, or the circus on a tiny tricycle. - Now that's entertainment.
jokes.setup: Fry suggesting monkey belongs in natural or circus environment
jokes.punchline: Bender enthusiastically supporting the exploitative option
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender moment - choosing the cruel entertainment option enthusiastically

jokes.text: I'm staying out of this. Now here's my opinion.
jokes.setup: Leela claiming she won't get involved
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicting herself
jokes.explanation: Perfect contradiction with economy - immediately undermines her stated position

jokes.text: Your watercraft's as ill-designed as you yourselves.
jokes.setup: Preppy fraternity member insulting Robot House
jokes.punchline: Overly formal insult about their boat and existence
jokes.explanation: Character-appropriate stuffy insult but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Eureka! The hat goes on the head! It's all so obvious now!
jokes.setup: Guenter struggling to put on intelligence hat during crisis
jokes.punchline: Treating basic hat placement as brilliant discovery
jokes.explanation: Good moment showing the transition from stupid to smart monkey

jokes.text: Eureka! The hat goes on the head! It's all so obvious now!
jokes.setup: Guenter putting on the intelligence hat in crisis
jokes.punchline: Treating basic hat placement as brilliant discovery
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist moment treating obvious as revelation

jokes.text: As a monkey, I was so dumb I tried to wear a hat on my butt.
jokes.setup: Guenter explaining his dissatisfaction with both states
jokes.punchline: Admitting to comically wrong hat placement
jokes.explanation: Good visual comedy and character insight into his confusion when unintelligent

jokes.text: So I'm transferring to business school!
jokes.setup: Guenter finding happiness as moderately intelligent
jokes.punchline: Choosing business school as the perfect fit for moderate intelligence
jokes.explanation: Sharp satire of business education as requiring only moderate intelligence

jokes.text: No!
jokes.setup: Guenter choosing business school over science
jokes.punchline: Professor's horror at this career choice
jokes.explanation: Good character reaction showing academic's horror at business school

jokes.text: GUENTER GOT HIS MBA AND BECAME PRESIDENT OF THE FOX NETWORK
jokes.setup: Epilogue text about Guenter's future
jokes.punchline: Becoming head of Fox Network (the show's own network) with just moderate intelligence
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke insulting their own network while maintaining the business school satire

jokes.text: GUENTER GOT HIS MBA AND BECAME PRESIDENT OF THE FOX NETWORK
jokes.setup: End credits revealing characters' fates
jokes.punchline: Self-referential joke about Fox network and MBA mediocrity
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke about the show's own network and MBA culture

jokes.text: HIS JOB DONE, BENDER STOLE EVERYTHING OF VALUE FROM ROBOT HOUSE AND RAN OFF
jokes.setup: Epilogue about Bender's fate
jokes.punchline: Perfect character consistency - helping then immediately stealing from them
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender ending - helps people then immediately betrays them, very character-specific

tmdb_overview: Fry, Leela, Bender and Farnsworth have to go to Mars University where Fry has to share a room with a talking monkey, Farnsworth's last invention. The monkey becomes a real headache when he starts beating Fry in all areas. Meanwhile, Bender is hanging out with nerd robots.
